data.title: 1st Source Q4 net income rises nearly 31% yr/yr
data.description:
Overview

  • Indiana financial institution's Q4 net income rose 30.87% yr/yr

  • Company's diluted EPS for Q4 increased 31.50% yr/yr

  • Average loans and leases grew 5.10% in 2025

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance for future quarters or full year

Result Drivers

  • LOAN AND LEASE GROWTH - Average loans and leases increased by $336.29 mln or 5.10% during 2025, driven by growth in renewable energy, commercial real estate, and construction equipment portfolios

  • NET INTEREST MARGIN EXPANSION - Tax-equivalent net interest margin expanded to 4.07% for 2025, up 43 basis points from 2024, due to higher rates on investment securities and stable loan rates

  • NONINTEREST INCOME DECLINE - Noninterest income fell 0.82% in 2025 due to $8.68 mln in losses from repositioning securities, offset by gains in renewable energy investments and increased advisory income

data.title: What dip? US stock traders turn eyes back towards highs
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes end green; Nasdaq out front, up ~0.9%
  • Comm Svcs leads S&P 500 sector gainers; Real Estate weakest group
  • Dollar slides; bitcoin dips; crude down ~2%; gold gains >1.5%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield flat at ~4.25%

WHAT DIP? US STOCK TRADERS TURN EYES BACK TOWARDS HIGHS

U.S. stocks posted their second strong day of gains on Thursday, and traders looked towards recapturing recent highs, as Tuesday’s jitters over new tariffs on Europe due to a dispute over Greenland appeared to be increasingly in the rear-view mirror.

Risk appetite improved after U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly stepped back from threats to impose tariffs as leverage to seize Greenland, ruled out the use of force and suggested a deal was in sight to end a dispute over the Danish territory.

“Greenland is likely to stay in the headlines in the near term, and markets remain susceptible to fresh political or geopolitical developments. But the latest stock rebound serves as a reminder that favorable fundamentals remain in the driver’s seat,” Ulrike Hoffmann-Burchardi, CIO Americas and global head of equities at UBS Global Wealth Management said on Thursday in a note.

All three major stock indexes ended up, with the Nasdaq Composite leading the rally with a 0.9% gain. Communication Services was the best-performing S&P 500 subsector, while real estate was the laggard.

Here is Thursday’s closing market snapshot:

data.title: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen Acquires 1 Million Shares
data.description:
Ryan Cohen, serving as President, CEO, and Chairman of GameStop Corp., has purchased a total of 1,000,000 shares of Class A Common Stock, with transactions on January 20 and 21, 2026, at weighted average prices of $21.1174 and $21.601 respectively. The total purchase amount was $21,359,200. Following these transactions, Cohen directly owns 38,347,842 shares of GameStop Corp. Additionally, Cohen received 3,734,784 Warrants from a board distribution on October 7, 2025, each allowing the purchase of one share at $32, expiring on October 30, 2026.

data.title: Chipmaker Intel beats Q4 revenue estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • Semiconductor maker's Q4 revenue fell 4% yr/yr but beat analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 was $0.15, despite a GAAP loss per share

  • Company introduced Intel 18A process technology, marking a key milestone

Outlook

  • Intel forecasts Q1 2026 revenue of $11.7 bln to $12.7 bln

  • Company expects Q1 2026 EPS of $(0.21) and non-GAAP EPS of $0.00

  • Intel anticipates supply improvements post-Q1 2026

Result Drivers

  • AI DEMAND - Strong demand for AI-related products contributed to Intel's Q4 performance, as noted by CEO Lip-Bu Tan

  • SUPPLY SHORTAGES - Intel navigated industry-wide supply shortages, impacting Q4 results, according to CFO David Zinsner

  • INTEL 18A TECHNOLOGY - Introduction of Intel 18A process technology marked a significant milestone, driving demand

data.title: Burke & Herbert Financial Services' Q4 net income rises to $30 mln
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. bank's Q4 net income rose to $30 mln, with EPS at $1.98

  • Company maintained strong balance sheet with $4.8 bln in liquidity

  • Company announced merger agreement with LINKBANCORP for expanded operations

Outlook

  • Burke & Herbert expects merger with LINKBANCORP to create $11 bln asset company

Result Drivers

  • LOAN PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT - Co exited $201.5 mln of non-strategic loans and originated $383.3 mln of new relationship-based loans, leading to a decrease in total gross loans

  • DEPOSIT STRATEGY - Core deposits increased by $51.9 mln, excluding a $60 mln decrease in brokered deposits, reflecting a shift towards more stable funding

  • INTEREST EXPENSE REDUCTION - Net interest income rose due to a $1.2 mln decrease in interest expense, driven by lower deposit costs

data.title: US Stocks Leap Amid Easing Geopolitical Fears
data.description:
US stocks rallied on Thursday as easing Greenland tensions and solid domestic data lifted sentiment, with the Nasdaq and the Dow gaining 0.7% each, while the S&P added 0.6% as earlier losses were retraced after President Trump stepped back from new tariff threats against Europe and pointed to a future framework agreement on Greenland.

Mega cap technology shares led the advance, with Meta up 5.7%, Alphabet gaining 0.8%, Microsoft rising 1.5%, Apple adding 0.3%, Amazon up 1.3%, and Nvidia higher by 0.8%.

Elsewhere, GE tumbled 7.4% despite beating earnings expectations, while Procter and Gamble climbed 2.7% even after missing on revenue.

The macro backdrop reinforced confidence after Q3 GDP was revised up to 4.4%, weekly jobless claims hovered near 200K, PCE inflation matched expectations and consumer spending remained resilient, easing concerns over the need for near term policy easing.

data.title: Trump calls for the prosecution of former US special counsel Jack Smith
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday called for former U.S. Special Counsel Jack Smith, who unsuccessfully prosecuted him, to be "prosecuted for his actions," saying on social media without providing evidence that Smith has committed "large scale perjury."

Trump said his comments were based on Smith's testimony before Congress earlier on Thursday. It was the first time he publicly testified since stepping down as special counsel last year ahead of Trump returning to the White House.

During that testimony, Smith said he believed that Trump's Justice Department will find some way to indict him.

data.title: Marathon Galveston Bay refinery reports operation issue with FCCU
data.description:
Marathon Petroleum reported on Thursday that a fluid catalytic cracking unit (FCCU) experienced operational issues at its 631,000‑barrel‑per‑day Galveston Bay refinery in Texas, according to a regulatory filing.

"The FCCU was de-pressured to the flare to minimize emissions," the filing added.
